heading: Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park boundary
label: 410yyy–3 Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park boundary
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the John D. Dingell, Jr. Conservation, Management, and Recreation Act, and not as part of act , which comprises this subchapter.June 14, 1934
Definition of “Secretary”Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 116–9section 1 of this title

children.heading: Historical park
children.label: 1 Historical park
children.text: The term “Historical Park” means the Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park in the State of Georgia, as redesignated by subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Map
children.label: 2 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Ocmulgee National Monument Proposed Boundary Adjustment”, numbered 363/125996, and dated January 2016.

children.heading: Study area
children.label: 3 Study area
children.text: The term “study area” means the Ocmulgee River corridor between the cities of Macon, Georgia, and Hawkinsville, Georgia.

children.heading: Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park
children.label: b Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Ocmulgee National Monument, established pursuant to sections 410yyy to 410yyy–2 of this title, shall be known and designated as the “Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park”.

children.heading: References
children.label: B References
children.text: Any reference in a law, map, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the “Ocmulgee National Monument” shall be deemed to be a reference to the “Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The boundary of the Historical Park is revised to include approximately 2,100 acres of land, as generally depicted on the map.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: B Availability of map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may acquire land and interests in land within the boundaries of the Historical Park by donation, purchase from a willing seller with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may not acquire by condemnation any land or interest in land within the boundaries of the Historical Park.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 4 Administration
children.text: The Secretary shall administer any land acquired under paragraph (3) as part of the Historical Park in accordance with applicable laws (including regulations).

children.heading: Ocmulgee River corridor special resource study
children.label: c Ocmulgee River corridor special resource study

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a special resource study of the study area.

children.label: A
children.text: evaluate the national significance of the study area;

children.label: B
children.text: determine the suitability and feasibility of designating the study area as a unit of the National Park System;

children.label: C
children.text: consider other alternatives for preservation, protection, and interpretation of the study area by the Federal Government, State or local government entities, or private and nonprofit organizations;

children.label: D
children.text: consult with interested Federal agencies, State or local governmental entities, private and nonprofit organizations, or any other interested individuals; and

children.label: E
children.text: identify cost estimates for any Federal acquisition, development, interpretation, operation, and maintenance associated with the alternatives.

children.heading: Applicable law
children.label: 3 Applicable law
children.text: The study required under paragraph (1) shall be conducted in accordance with .section 100507 of title 54

children.label: A
children.text: the results of the study; and

children.label: B
children.text: any conclusions and recommendations of the Secretary.
